description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Georgia (  JOR-jə) is a state in the Southeastern, South Atlantic, and Deep South regions of the United States.

description: American political party
description_wiki: The Democratic Party is the major liberal political party in the United States, sitting on the center to center-left of the political spectrum.

aliases: Republican Party of the United States
description: American political party
description_wiki: The Republican Party, commonly known as the Grand Old Party (GOP), is the major conservative and right-wing political party in the United States.

description: organization
description_wiki: The National Association of Insurance Commissioners (NAIC) is the U.S. standard-setting and regulatory support organization created and governed by the chief insurance regulators from the 50 states, the District of Columbia, and five U.S. territories.

description: American politician (1902–2003)
description_wiki: James Strom Thurmond Sr.

description: American politician (1904-1986)
American politician (1904-1982)
description_wiki: Clifford Philip Case Jr.

description: Chief Justice of the Supreme Court of the United States from 1953 to 1969 (1891–1974)
description_wiki: Earl Warren (March 19, 1891 – July 9, 1974) was an American attorney and politician who served as the 30th governor of California from 1943 to 1953, and as the 14th chief justice of the United States from 1953 to 1969.

description: president of the United States from 1945 to 1953; politician (1884–1972)
description_wiki: Harry S. Truman  (May 8, 1884 – December 26, 1972) was the 33rd president of the United States, serving from 1945 to 1953.

description: president of the United States from 1953 to 1961 (1890–1969)
description_wiki: Dwight David "Ike" Eisenhower (born David Dwight Eisenhower; October 14, 1890 – March 28, 1969) was the 34th president of the United States, serving from 1953 to 1961.

description: president of the United States from 1961 to 1963 (1917–1963)
description_wiki: John Fitzgerald Kennedy (May 29, 1917 – November 22, 1963), also known as JFK, was the 35th president of the United States, serving from 1961 until his assassination in 1963.

description: president of the United States from 1963 to 1969 (1908–1973)
description_wiki: Lyndon Baines Johnson (August 27, 1908 – January 22, 1973), also known as LBJ, was the 36th president of the United States from 1963 until 1969.

description: president of the United States from 1969 to 1974 (1913–1994)
description_wiki: Richard Milhous Nixon (January 9, 1913 – April 22, 1994) was the 37th president of the United States, serving from 1969 until his resignation in 1974.

description: political scandal that occurred in the United States (1972–1974)
description_wiki: The Watergate scandal, or simply Watergate, was a political scandal in the United States involving the administration of President Richard Nixon.

description: American lawyer, politician
description_wiki: John Wesley Dean III (born October 14, 1938) is a disbarred American attorney who served as White House Counsel for U.S. President Richard Nixon from July 1970 until April 1973.

description: president of the United States from 1974 to 1977 (1913–2006)
description_wiki: Gerald Rudolph Ford Jr.

aliases: the Union of Soviet Socialist Republics
description: former country in Eurasia (1922–1991)
description_wiki: The Union of Soviet Socialist Republics (USSR), also known as the Soviet Union, was a transcontinental country that spanned much of Eurasia from 1922 until its dissolution in 1991.

description: country in East Asia
country located primarily in North America
description_wiki: The United States of America (USA), also known as the United States (U.S.) or America, is a country primarily located in North America.

description: vice president of the United States from 1965 to 1969
description_wiki: Hubert Horatio Humphrey Jr.

description: United States Senator from Massachusetts from 1962 to 2009
description_wiki: Edward Moore Kennedy (February 22, 1932 – August 25, 2009) was an American lawyer and politician who represented Massachusetts in the United States Senate from 1962 until his death in 2009.

description: car accident in 1969 on Chappaquiddick Island
description_wiki: The Chappaquiddick incident occurred on Chappaquiddick Island, Massachusetts, United States, sometime around midnight between July 18 and 19, 1969, when Mary Jo Kopechne died inside the car driven by United States Senator Ted Kennedy after he accidentally drove off a narrow bridge into Poucha Pond.

description: upper house of the United States Congress
description_wiki: The United States Senate is a chamber of the bicameral United States Congress; it is the upper house, and the U.S. House of Representatives is the lower house.
